output.one_sentence_summary: [ch01 - TITLE I—AGRICULTURE] This section allocates over $100 billion to fund forest restoration, rural development, and agricultural research, with a focus on climate resilience, wildfire prevention, and support for underserved communities. [ch02 - Subtitle G—Medicaid] This section establishes a new federal Medicaid program to close the coverage gap in states that have not expanded Medicaid, providing health benefits to low-income individuals through contracts with managed care organizations and third-party administrators. [ch03 - Subtitle C—Encouraging Small] This section establishes programs to support small businesses through grants, funding for innovation ecosystems, and expanded access to capital and investment. [ch04 - Subtitle G—Green Energy] This section extends and modifies tax credits for renewable energy, clean hydrogen, electric vehicles, and other green technologies, while adding new requirements for domestic content, labor standards, and environmental justice. [ch05 - Subtitle I—Responsibly Funding] This section increases corporate and individual tax rates, limits deductions for interest and certain business expenses, strengthens international tax rules, and imposes new taxes on high-income individuals and large retirement accounts.
bullets: [ch01] Allocates $10 billion for hazardous fuels reduction projects in the wildland-urban interface to protect communities from wildfires.
[ch01] Provides $4.5 billion for vegetation management and forest restoration projects on National Forest System land, prioritizing ecological integrity and climate adaptation.
[ch01] Authorizes $2.25 billion for a Civilian Climate Corps to support forest management and restoration on both federal and non-federal lands.
[ch01] Dedicates $9 billion to grants for non-federal forest restoration and fuels reduction, including community wildfire protection and post-fire recovery.
[ch01] Appropriates $1.25 billion for the Forest Legacy Program to acquire land with high carbon sequestration potential and $3 billion for urban and community forestry initiatives.
[ch01] Allocates $400 million for rural business and water programs, including grants for infrastructure and loan subsidies in persistent poverty counties.
[ch01] Provides $811.75 million for the Rural Energy for America Program to support renewable energy and energy efficiency projects in rural areas.
[ch01] Establishes a $9.7 billion Clean Energy Repowering Program to assist rural utilities in retiring high-emission facilities and transitioning to clean energy.
[ch02] Creates a new federal Medicaid program to provide health benefits to individuals in non-expansion states who would otherwise qualify for Medicaid, with coverage starting January 1, 2025.

[ch02] Authorizes the Secretary to contract with Medicaid managed care organizations or third-party administrators to deliver benefits, with contracts based on competitive bidding and performance standards.
[ch02] Sets minimum federal standards for benefits, beneficiary protections, access to care, and redeterminations, ensuring parity with state Medicaid programs.
[ch02] Requires the federal program to offer benchmark or equivalent coverage, prohibit cost-sharing, and apply key Medicaid provisions like managed care and drug rebate rules.
[ch02] Establishes a data-matching system to verify income and ensure eligibility, with protections for individuals whose income changes during the year.
[ch02] Imposes penalties for non-compliance, including payment withholding, corrective action plans, and civil monetary penalties.
[ch02] Requires states that previously expanded Medicaid but later stopped to make payments to the federal government to offset the cost of the federal program.
[ch02] Provides for transitions from exchange plans to the federal program and from the federal program to state plans when states begin providing coverage.
[ch03] Creates a $400 million Growth Accelerator Competition to fund eligible entities that help new small businesses access capital, mentors, and business advice.
[ch03] Establishes a $525 million National Innovation Support Ecosystem Network to fund projects that support training, mentorship, and business incubation for innovative startups.

[ch03] Authorizes $9.5 billion for a new Venture Small Business Investment Company (SBIC) Facility to increase equity investment in underserved communities, infrastructure, and small businesses owned by veterans, individuals with disabilities, and members of underrepresented communities.
[ch03] Creates a $20 million Emerging Managers Program to help new managers start small business investment companies with increased leverage and reduced regulatory barriers.

[ch03] Establishes a $40 million Microcap SBIC License program to allow applicants with less investment experience to obtain licenses, with a focus on underserved communities and minority-serving institutions.
[ch03] Provides $2.5 million for outreach and education to help potential applicants understand the SBIC licensing process.

[ch03] Creates a $2 million SBIC Working Group to develop recommendations for increasing the number of SBICs in underfinanced and underlicensed states and improving access to capital for minority- and women-owned businesses.
[ch04] Extends and modifies the renewable electricity production credit and energy credit through 2034, with phased reductions in credit amounts based on construction start dates.
[ch04] Introduces new tax credits for clean hydrogen production, zero-emission nuclear power, and zero-emission facilities, with specific eligibility and domestic content requirements.
[ch04] Creates a new credit for solar facilities in low-income communities, with increased benefits and environmental justice allocation limits.
[ch04] Establishes a new credit for qualified commercial electric vehicles and alternative fuel refueling property, with labor and domestic content requirements.
[ch04] Adds new requirements for prevailing wages, apprenticeship participation, and domestic content for most green energy projects, with exceptions for cost increases or supply shortages.
[ch04] Introduces a new credit for energy storage technology, biogas property, and microgrid controllers, expanding the scope of eligible clean energy investments.
[ch04] Creates a new credit for qualified environmental justice programs at educational institutions, with funding limits and selection criteria based on community impact.
[ch04] Extends and modifies tax credits for biodiesel, sustainable aviation fuel, and other renewable fuels, with new incentives for low-carbon aviation fuel.

[ch05] Increases the corporate tax rate to 18% for income up to $400,000, 21% for income between $400,000 and $5 million, and 26.5% for income above $5 million, with an additional 3% surcharge for corporations with income over $10 million.
[ch05] Limits the deduction for dividends received by corporations from 50% to 60% and adjusts the rules for controlled groups of corporations to prevent tax avoidance.
[ch05] Imposes a new limit on interest deductions for certain international financial reporting groups, requiring that interest deductions not exceed 110% of net interest expense.

[ch05] Increases the tax rate on high-income individuals to 39.6% for income over $450,000 (for married couples), $425,000 (for heads of households), and $400,000 (for single filers), with a 25% capital gains rate for high earners.
[ch05] Imposes a 3% surcharge on the modified adjusted gross income of high-income individuals, estates, and trusts exceeding $5 million (or $2.5 million for married couples filing separately).

[ch05] Strengthens international tax rules by requiring foreign tax credits to be calculated country-by-country, limiting carrybacks of foreign losses, and expanding the definition of global intangible low-taxed income.
[ch05] Imposes new excise taxes on certain high-priced drugs during periods of noncompliance with negotiated pricing agreements and establishes a Fair Price Negotiation Program to lower drug prices.
[ch05] Increases the tax on certain tobacco products and imposes a new tax on nicotine used in vaping and other products.

usc_data.heading: Certain residents of possessions considered citizens of the United States
usc_data.text_excerpt: A decedent who was a citizen of the United States and a resident of a possession thereof at the time of his death shall, for purposes of the tax imposed by this chapter, be considered a “citizen” of the United States within the meaning of that term wherever used in this title unless he acquired his United States citizenship solely by reason of (1) his being a citizen of such possession of the United States, or (2) his birth or residence within such possession of the United States.

citations.bill_context: Section 130001 adds a new title XXII to the Social Security Act, including section 2209 on funding for state administration of legacy programs.

usc_data.heading: Certain residents of possessions considered nonresidents not citizens of the United States
usc_data.text_excerpt: A decedent who was a citizen of the United States and a resident of a possession thereof at the time of his death shall, for purposes of the tax imposed by this chapter, be considered a “nonresident not a citizen of the United States” within the meaning of that term wherever used in this title, but only if such person acquired his United States citizenship solely by reason of (1) his being a citizen of such possession of the United States, or (2) his birth or residence within such possession of the United States.

usc_data.heading: Definition of executor

usc_data.heading: Certain residents of possessions considered citizens of the United States
usc_data.text_excerpt: A decedent who was a citizen of the United States and a resident of a possession thereof at the time of his death shall, for purposes of the tax imposed by this chapter, be considered a “citizen” of the United States within the meaning of that term wherever used in this title unless he acquired his United States citizenship solely by reason of (1) his being a citizen of such possession of the United States, or (2) his birth or residence within such possession of the United States.

citations.bill_context: Section 130001 adds a new title XXII to the Social Security Act, including section 2209 on funding for state administration of legacy programs.

usc_data.heading: Certain residents of possessions considered nonresidents not citizens of the United States
usc_data.text_excerpt: A decedent who was a citizen of the United States and a resident of a possession thereof at the time of his death shall, for purposes of the tax imposed by this chapter, be considered a “nonresident not a citizen of the United States” within the meaning of that term wherever used in this title, but only if such person acquired his United States citizenship solely by reason of (1) his being a citizen of such possession of the United States, or (2) his birth or residence within such possession of the United States.

(b) Publicly traded partnership For purposes of this section, the term “publicly traded partnership” means any partnership if— (1) interests in such partnership are traded on an established securities market, or (2) interests in such partnership are readily tradable on a secondary market (or the substantial equivalent thereof). headlines.headline: Wildfire Shield: $10B for At-Risk Communities
headlines.hook: A new $10 billion fund targets hazardous fuels reduction in the wildland-urban interface, directly protecting thousands of homes and lives.
headlines.target_audience: Rural homeowners, fire prevention advocates, climate policy watchers

headlines.headline: Tribal Lands Get $9B in Forest Restoration Cash
headlines.hook: Federal funding for forest restoration now explicitly prioritizes tribal sovereignty and community-led projects, sparking debate over equitable distribution.
headlines.target_audience: Indigenous rights advocates, federal policy critics, environmental justice groups

headlines.headline: Farmers’ New Climate Corps: 10,000 Jobs in the Woods
headlines.hook: The Civilian Climate Corps will deploy thousands of young workers to restore forests—offering rural youth a lifeline and a green career path.
headlines.target_audience: Young rural workers, environmental educators, workforce development experts

headlines.headline: Rural Utilities Get $9.7B to Ditch Coal
headlines.hook: A bold clean energy transition for rural power providers—fueled by a $9.7 billion repowering program to retire polluting plants.
headlines.target_audience: Rural utility managers, clean energy investors, Democratic lawmakers

headlines.headline: Post-Fire Recovery: $9B for Burned Communities
headlines.hook: After devastating wildfires, a $9 billion grant stream kicks in—offering hope, but also a race against bureaucratic delays.
headlines.target_audience: Fire survivors, local government officials, emergency responders

headlines.headline: Hidden Clause: Forests Must Now Protect Water
headlines.hook: A little-known provision forces forest management to prioritize watershed health—rewriting how land is managed across the West.
headlines.target_audience: Environmental lawyers, hydrologists, land-use planners

headlines.headline: Rural Water Grants: $400M for Aging Infrastructure
headlines.hook: New rules mandate that rural water systems must apply for grants to fix failing pipes—under a $400 million federal program.
headlines.target_audience: Small-town water managers, public works officials, infrastructure reformers

headlines.headline: Urban Trees Get $3B—Even in Poor Neighborhoods
headlines.hook: Federal green space funding now targets urban heat islands in low-income cities, turning climate policy into a tool for racial equity.
headlines.target_audience: Urban planners, climate equity activists, city council members

headlines.headline: Climate Corps: A New Frontier for Conservation
headlines.hook: The Civilian Climate Corps is launching with $2.25 billion—but oversight groups are already demanding transparency on hiring and project tracking.
headlines.target_audience: Government accountability advocates, watchdog journalists, federal auditors

headlines.headline: Forest Legacy Program: $1.25B for Carbon Capture
headlines.hook: A little-reported $1.25 billion program will buy land with high carbon sequestration potential—turning forests into climate assets.
headlines.target_audience: Climate scientists, carbon market investors, land conservationists

headlines.headline: Federal Medicaid for Non-Expansion States
headlines.hook: A new federal program will cover low-income residents in states that refused Medicaid expansion—starting January 2025.
headlines.target_audience: Budget watchers and fiscal policy analysts
evidence_provisions: Creates a new federal Medicaid program to provide health benefits to individuals in non-expansion states

headlines.headline: Medicaid Expansion via Federal Backstop
headlines.hook: The federal government steps in where states refused to expand Medicaid—sparking a political showdown over federal overreach.
headlines.target_audience: Political commentators and state policy skeptics
evidence_provisions: Creates a new federal Medicaid program to provide health benefits to individuals in non-expansion states
Requires states that previously expanded Medicaid but later stopped to make payments to the federal government

headlines.headline: Low-Income Patients Get Federal Coverage
headlines.hook: Thousands of uninsured people in non-expansion states will finally get health coverage through a federal safety net.
headlines.target_audience: Advocates for low-income families and healthcare access
evidence_provisions: Provides health benefits to low-income individuals in non-expansion states who would otherwise qualify for Medicaid

headlines.headline: States That Backtracked Must Pay Up
headlines.hook: States that expanded Medicaid and then reversed course now face financial penalties to fund the federal fallback program.
headlines.target_audience: State legislators and Medicaid policy wonks
evidence_provisions: Requires states that previously expanded Medicaid but later stopped to make payments to the federal government to offset the cost of the federal program

headlines.headline: Federal Contracts with Managed Care Firms
headlines.hook: A high-stakes bidding war looms as federal contractors race to win Medicaid delivery contracts in non-expansion states.
headlines.target_audience: Healthcare industry insiders and contract bid watchers
evidence_provisions: Authorizes the Secretary to contract with Medicaid managed care organizations or third-party administrators to deliver benefits
Contracts based on competitive bidding and performance standards

headlines.headline: No Cost-Sharing for Federal Medicaid Recipients
headlines.hook: A little-known rule ensures that enrollees in the federal Medicaid program pay nothing out-of-pocket—no premiums, no copays.
headlines.target_audience: Consumer advocates and health equity researchers
evidence_provisions: Sets minimum federal standards for benefits, beneficiary protections, access to care, and redeterminations

headlines.headline: Data Matching to Catch Income Changes
headlines.hook: A new federal data-matching system will track income fluctuations to keep people enrolled when their earnings shift.
headlines.target_audience: Administrative law experts and compliance officers
evidence_provisions: Establishes a data-matching system to verify income and ensure eligibility
With protections for individuals whose income changes during the year

headlines.headline: Penalties for Failed Medicaid Delivery
headlines.hook: States or contractors that fail to meet federal standards risk losing payments and facing fines.
headlines.target_audience: Government oversight groups and accountability advocates
evidence_provisions: Imposes penalties for non-compliance, including payment withholding, corrective action plans, and civil monetary penalties

headlines.headline: Transition from Exchange to Federal Medicaid
headlines.hook: A hidden pathway lets people on ACA exchanges switch to federal Medicaid without losing coverage—starting in 2025.
headlines.target_audience: Health insurance brokers and enrollment counselors
evidence_provisions: Provides for transitions from exchange plans to the federal program

headlines.headline: Federal Medicaid Follows State Expansion
headlines.hook: The federal program is designed to seamlessly hand over coverage to states when they finally expand Medicaid.
headlines.target_audience: Federal-state policy coordinators and intergovernmental analysts
evidence_provisions: Provides for transitions from the federal program to state plans when states begin providing coverage

headlines.headline: SBICs Get $9.5B for Underserved Communities
headlines.hook: A massive $9.5 billion infusion into small business investment companies is designed to reshape capital access for marginalized entrepreneurs.
headlines.target_audience: Economic policy analysts, venture capital investors, community development advocates
evidence_provisions: Section 131102(a) adds a new paragraph (16) to section 401(k)
Authorizes $9.5 billion for a new Venture Small Business Investment Company (SBIC) Facility

headlines.headline: New SBIC License for First-Time Managers
headlines.hook: A new program lets inexperienced managers launch SBICs with reduced barriers—raising questions about oversight and risk in federal funding.
headlines.target_audience: Regulatory watchdogs, financial compliance experts, small business regulators
evidence_provisions: Creates a $20 million Emerging Managers Program
Establishes a $40 million Microcap SBIC License program

headlines.headline: Veterans, Disabled Entrepreneurs Get Priority Access
headlines.hook: For the first time, veterans and people with disabilities are explicitly prioritized in federal small business funding programs.
headlines.target_audience: Veteran service organizations, disability advocacy groups, minority entrepreneurs
evidence_provisions: Authorizes $9.5 billion for a new Venture Small Business Investment Company (SBIC) Facility
Focus on small businesses owned by veterans, individuals with disabilities, and members of underrepresented communities

headlines.headline: SBICs in Rural States Get Special Boost
headlines.hook: A new working group aims to fix the geographic imbalance in small business capital—targeting underlicensed states with no SBICs.
headlines.target_audience: Rural policymakers, state legislators, regional economic development officers
evidence_provisions: Creates a $2 million SBIC Working Group
To develop recommendations for increasing the number of SBICs in underfinanced and underlicensed states

headlines.headline: Hidden $40M Program for Minority-Serving Institutions
headlines.hook: A little-known $40 million program quietly opens SBIC licensing to minority-serving institutions with minimal experience.
headlines.target_audience: Grant writers, minority business leaders, higher education administrators
evidence_provisions: Establishes a $40 million Microcap SBIC License program
With a focus on underserved communities and minority-serving institutions

headlines.headline: New Tax Credit for Automatic Retirement Plans
headlines.hook: A new tax credit is being created to incentivize small employers to adopt automatic retirement contributions—changing the rules for workplace savings.
headlines.target_audience: HR professionals, small business owners, retirement plan providers
evidence_provisions: To provide a credit for certain small employer automatic retirement arrangements

headlines.headline: SBICs Can Now Fund Rural Innovation Hubs
headlines.hook: Federal funds are being funneled into rural innovation ecosystems, potentially reshaping economic power beyond urban centers.
headlines.target_audience: Rural development strategists, regional planners, innovation economists
evidence_provisions: Establishes a $525 million National Innovation Support Ecosystem Network
To fund projects that support training, mentorship, and business incubation for innovative startups

headlines.headline: New $400M Competition for Startup Accelerators
headlines.hook: A high-stakes $400 million race is underway—entities across the country are scrambling to win grants for helping new small businesses.
headlines.target_audience: Startup accelerators, incubator directors, nonprofit leaders
evidence_provisions: Creates a $400 million Growth Accelerator Competition
To fund eligible entities that help new small businesses access capital, mentors, and business advice

headlines.headline: Tax Refunds Can Now Go Directly to IRAs
headlines.hook: A new rule allows tax refunds to be deposited directly into IRAs—raising concerns about unintended consequences for low-income savers.
headlines.target_audience: Consumer finance advocates, IRS oversight groups, tax equity researchers
evidence_provisions: To allow tax refunds to be directly deposited into an IRA

headlines.headline: SBIC Licensing Rules Are Being Overhauled
headlines.hook: Behind closed doors, a new working group is crafting sweeping changes to SBIC licensing—set to unlock access for thousands of new players.
headlines.target_audience: Financial regulators, policy insiders, emerging fund managers
evidence_provisions: Creates a $2 million SBIC Working Group
To develop recommendations for increasing the number of SBICs in underfinanced and underlicensed states

headlines.headline: Solar Deadline Extended to 2034
headlines.hook: Renewable energy developers now have until 2034 to get solar projects online and qualify for major tax credits.
headlines.target_audience: renewable energy investors and project developers

headlines.headline: Wind Projects Get 10-Year Extension
headlines.hook: A last-minute extension for wind facilities has sparked debate over whether it favors big developers over new entrants.
headlines.target_audience: energy policy analysts and environmental advocates

headlines.headline: Clean Hydrogen Gets Its Own Credit
headlines.hook: A new tax credit for clean hydrogen production is quietly becoming a game-changer for industrial decarbonization.
headlines.target_audience: clean tech investors and industrial policy watchers

headlines.headline: Low-Income Solar Projects Get Boost
headlines.hook: Low-income communities will now receive enhanced tax credits for solar facilities—finally putting green energy within reach.
headlines.target_audience: community organizers and environmental justice advocates

headlines.headline: EVs Now Get Labor Bonus Credit
headlines.hook: Electric vehicle manufacturers must now meet wage and apprenticeship standards—or lose key tax breaks.
headlines.target_audience: labor unions and auto industry stakeholders

headlines.headline: Domestic Steel Requirement for Solar
headlines.hook: Solar projects must now use U.S.-made steel or lose their tax credit—raising costs and reshaping supply chains.
headlines.target_audience: construction firms and materials suppliers

headlines.headline: Apprenticeship Mandate for Clean Energy
headlines.hook: A new apprenticeship rule could delay projects—but promises long-term workforce transformation.
headlines.target_audience: union leaders and project managers

headlines.headline: Energy Storage Now Tax-Eligible
headlines.hook: The new credit for energy storage tech could reduce U.S. reliance on foreign battery imports.
headlines.target_audience: national security and energy security analysts

headlines.headline: Green Schools Get Environmental Justice Funds
headlines.hook: Educational institutions in underserved areas can now access federal green grants—raising scrutiny over allocation fairness.
headlines.target_audience: nonprofit watchdogs and education equity groups

headlines.headline: Aviation Fuel Credit Targets Carbon
headlines.hook: A little-known credit for low-carbon aviation fuel could reshape the future of air travel emissions.
headlines.target_audience: sustainable aviation experts and airline executives

headlines.headline: Corporate Taxes Jump to 26.5% Above $5M
headlines.hook: A new tiered corporate tax structure targets the largest firms, with rates soaring to 26.5% for income over $5 million.
headlines.target_audience: Business leaders, financial analysts, policy wonks

headlines.headline: High Earners Face 3% Surcharge Over $5M
headlines.hook: The wealthiest Americans now pay a 3% surcharge on income exceeding $5 million—sparking debate over fairness and incentives.
headlines.target_audience: Progressive voters, tax reform advocates

headlines.headline: Big Pharma Hit with New Drug Pricing Penalties
headlines.hook: Pharmaceutical companies that fail to comply with negotiated drug prices now face steep excise taxes—potentially lowering costs for patients.
headlines.target_audience: Patients, healthcare advocates, public health professionals

headlines.headline: Tobacco Tax Surge Targets Vaping Boom
headlines.hook: A new tax on nicotine in vaping products signals a political shift toward regulating youth-friendly tobacco alternatives.
headlines.target_audience: Public health policymakers, youth advocacy groups

headlines.headline: Interest Deductions Capped at 110% of Expense
headlines.hook: A little-known cap on interest deductions could quietly reshape corporate debt strategies across international financial groups.
headlines.target_audience: Tax attorneys, CFOs, international finance teams

headlines.headline: Dividend Deductions Rise to 60% for Big Firms
headlines.hook: The 60% cap on dividend deductions for large corporations could alter how multinationals structure their intercompany payouts.
headlines.target_audience: Corporate tax planners, compliance officers

headlines.headline: Global Tax Rules Now Country-by-Country
headlines.hook: Foreign tax credits must now be calculated country-by-country—reshaping how multinational firms manage global tax liabilities.
headlines.target_audience: International tax experts, global business executives

headlines.headline: Retirement Accounts Face New 3% Tax
headlines.hook: Ultra-high-net-worth individuals could see their retirement savings taxed at 3%—a rare move that’s already igniting backlash.
headlines.target_audience: Wealthy retirees, financial planners

headlines.headline: Corporate Interest Carryforwards Now Limited
headlines.hook: The new rules on disallowed interest carryforwards are designed to prevent corporations from gaming tax systems over time.
headlines.target_audience: Tax watchdogs, investigative journalists

headlines.headline: Controlled Groups Face Tighter Tax Rules
headlines.hook: A little-noticed amendment to 26 USC 1561 could crack down on tax avoidance through corporate family structures.
headlines.target_audience: Tax reform insiders, legal analysts
